content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(716) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901           322-6441          322-6444

September 25, 1979

Lieutenant Governor Mike Curb
Office of the Lieutenant Governor
State Capitol, Room 1028
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Lieutenant Governor Curb:

     Thank you for your letter dated August 14, 1979 regarding your disclosure obligations under Section 87200 et seq. of the Political Reform Act.

     First, you have asked what financial holdings, if any, that are being held in a blind trust agreement between you and the Bank of America as trustee must be reported. Government Code Section 87200 et seq. requires you to disclose all investments, interests in real property and sources of income. Sections 82030, 82033 and 82034 which define these terms provide that investments, real property, and income also includes those held by a trust in which you have a 10% interest or greater. The Commission has provided by regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code 18235, that certain blind trusts may operate to limit the disclosure obligation of public officials.

     Because your blind trust complies with all the provisions of Regulation 18235, you need not report your share of investments, interests in real property, and income that are acquired by the trust, after the trust agreement goes into effect. However, you must report the existence of the blind trust pursuant to 18235 on your Statement of Economic Interests as well as those original assets placed in the trust until they are disposed of by the trust. Your Statement also should contain the date of disposal of any original asset and the purchaser of the asset unless the purchaser's identity is exempt from disclosure by Regulation 18729 (enclosed).

Lieutenant Governor Mike Curb
September 25, 1979
Page Two


The second question you have asked us to consider is
whether you must report interests held by your company's
pension plan. Your letter does not contain sufficient in-
formation to provide an answer to your question. Ultimately,
the matter will turn on whether your interest in the plan is
10% or greater by virtue of your interest in the company
which may be trustor of the fund or as an employee who is
a beneficiary. If your interest in the fund is 10% or
greater, disclosure and disqualification regarding assets
held by the fund would be required.

I hope that this advice is helpful. If you have any
questions please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Dwight E. Dickerson
Legal Counsel
Conflicts of Interest
Division

Enclosure
DED:nrw

MIKE CURB
LIEUTENANT GOVERNOR

State of California
Office of the Lieutenant Governor

August 14, 1979

Fair Practice Political Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attn: Dwight Dickerson

Dear Sir;

Enclosed is a copy of Article III, "Creation
of and Provisions Governing Blind Trust", of a
trust agreement between Bank of America and me.
Please advise if it will be necessary for me
to report any of the holdings held by the trustee.
Also, I am the owner of 100% of the stock
of MIKE CURB PRODUCTIONS, INC. Which company
has a pension plan for its employees? My father
is trustee of the plan. It will also be appreciated
if you will let me know if I must report property
held in the pension plan.

Sincerely,

Mike Curb

MIKE CURB

Not printed or mailed at government expense

ARTICLE II:     DEFINITIONS


     2.1  In this instrument, the term "issue" shall refer to
lineal descendants of all degrees.


     2.2  In this instrument, the terms "child," "children,"
and "issue" shall include adopted children who were minors at the
date of adoption.


     2.3  In this instrument, the terms "incompetent" and
"incompetence" shall refer to a legally determined incapacity.


     2.4  Whenever provision is made to pay for the education
of a beneficiary, the term "education" shall include collegiate, vocational, and postgraduate study as long as in the TRUSTEE'S discretion
it is pursued to advantage by the beneficiary at an institution of
the beneficiary's choice.  In determining payments to be made to the
beneficiary for such education, the TRUSTEE shall consider the bene-
ficiary's reasonably related living and traveling expenses.


     2.5  As used in this instrument, the masculine, feminine,
or neuter gender, and the singular or plural number shall each
include the others whenever the context so indicates.  Headings of
Articles in this instrument are included for convenience of refer-
ence only and are not to be considered in the construction of any
provision of this instrument.


               ARTICLE III:  CREATION OF AND PROVISIONS
                            GOVERNING BLIND TRUST


     3.1  By reason of the election of the TRUSTOR to State
political office, the TRUSTOR desires that this trust be a blind
trust which complies with the provisions of 2 Cal. Adm. C §18235.
Therefore, the following provisions shall be in effect during the
time of the TRUSTOR's government service.


                                    - 2 -

3.1.1   In accordance with the provisions of Article
VII, the TRUSTEE shall have sole authority to manage the trust estate,
including the power to invest and reinvest the assets of the trust
estate and including the power to dispose of and acquire trust
assets, as the TRUSTEE, in the TRUSTEE'S sole discretion, deems
advisable, and the TRUSTEE is expressly prohibited from consulting
or notifying the TRUSTOR regarding such actions.


3.1.2   The above provisions notwithstanding, the
TRUSTEE is directed to notify the TRUSTOR in writing, prior to
March 1 of each year, of the date of disposition and value a dis-
position of any original investment or interest in real property
transferred to this trust.


3.1.3   The TRUSTEE is prohibited from disclosing to
the TRUSTOR any information concerning the assets of this trust,
except for the information required under subparagraph 3.1.2.


3.1.4   The TRUSTEE shall deliver to the TRUSTOR,
prior to March 1 of each year, a statement signed under penalty of
perjury indicating that it has not revealed to the TRUSTOR any
information regarding the trust except that required pursuant to
subparagraph 3.1.2, and further stating that the trust is in com-
pliance with 2 Cal. Adm. C §18235.


3.2   This Article shall cease to be operative when the
TRUSTOR's government service ceases or when the TRUSTOR delivers to
the TRUSTEE written notice that the TRUSTOR no longer wishes the
trust to comply with 2 Cal. Adm. C §18235, except that the TRUSTEE
shall have sole authority to manage the trust estate, including the
power to invest and reinvest the assets of the trust estate as the TRUSTEE,
in the TRUSTEE'S sole discretion, deems advisable in accordance with
the provisions of ARTICLE VII.


ARTICLE IV:   PROVISIONS RELATING TO THE
DEATH OF THE TRUSTOR


4.1   On the death of the TRUSTOR, the estate and inheritance
taxes, including interest and penalties, attributable to the trust
estate arising because of the TRUSTOR'S death shall be paid by the
Executor named in the Last Will and Testament of the TRUSTOR from his
probate estate.

- 3 -

sections.question: Please advise if it will be necessary for me to report any of the holdings held by the trustee. Also, I am the owner of 100% of the stock of MIKE CURB PRODUCTIONS, INC. Which company has a pension plan for its employees? My father is trustee of the plan. It will also be appreciated if you will let me know if I must report property held in the pension plan.
sections.question_synthetic: Must Lieutenant Governor Mike Curb report holdings in a blind trust managed by Bank of America, and must he report property held in a pension plan of his wholly-owned company where his father serves as trustee?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Mike Curb to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document includes trust agreement Article III as referenced attachment. No FPPC conclusion or advice is present in this document. High confidence in extraction of question and basic structure, though full trust agreement context is included.

embedding.summary: Mike Curb, California Lieutenant Governor, submitted an inquiry to the FPPC requesting guidance on reporting requirements for assets held in a blind trust with Bank of America and for property held in a pension plan of his wholly-owned company (Mike Curb Productions, Inc.) where his father is trustee.
